title: fix(deployer): resolve cross-package tsconfig path alias deps in dev

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix: resolve TypeScript path aliases in transpiled workspace packages
fix(deployer): support tsconfig paths when using extended configs

relatedIssues.title: [BUG] mastra dev fails to build package aliases in Mono Repo

summary: When tsconfig path aliases (e.g. @lib/*) point to files in a different monorepo package, transitive dependencies of those aliased files (e.g. better-auth) were unresolvable at runtime from .mastra/output/. The fix adds a fallback in the tsconfig-paths Rollup plugin: when a bare import can't be resolved normally and the importer was itself resolved via a tsconfig alias, it uses createRequire(importer) to resolve the dependency from the importer's location. A regression test covering the cross-package scenario is included. The changeset is present.
maintainerNotes: Approved by abhiaiyer91 (org member). CI checks are stuck in PENDING state — 7 test suites (Core, E2E, Memory, RAG, MCP, Tool Builder, Combined store) have been pending since Feb 26 and appear to never have run, likely due to missing secrets/authorization for the fork. Vercel deploys also failed (authorization required). The code itself looks correct and well-scoped (15 lines of logic + 75 lines of test). One concern flagged by CodeRabbit: the hardcoded hono exception (request !== 'hono' && !request.startsWith('hono/')) is duplicated from the existing code path above — this is a pre-existing pattern, not introduced by this PR, but worth noting. The author is a repeat contributor who previously had PR #11788 merged on the same plugin. A maintainer should re-trigger CI from the base repo to validate tests pass before merging.
